title: More Raids
description: A vanilla+ datapack that adds four brand-new raids with powerful enemies to conquer and rewarding loot to win!
body:
![More Raids Header](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/6ddde2316576c528c5083f69ec015775c6a130de.png) **More Raids is a Vanilla-Plus styled datapack that adds a variety of new wave-based raids for you to fight and conquer!** *You can obtain the Spawning Items either through crafting or through the give functions:* `/function give:` *The recipes can be found in the recipe book after picking up a core mob drop (rotten flesh, bones, gunpowder, or string).* ![The Putrid Heart, Brittle Bone, and Unstable TNT](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/d03c48a620a7ce84f90f2a654b47be2f2c86f055.png)| The new raids function very similarly to the vanilla Pillager Raid. You can spawn each raid by using a special spawning item at night. Once a raid has been spawned, you have until the sunrise to kill all monsters and defeat all waves! ---|--- **Each raid offers a unique challenge presented by its main monster. Zombie Seiges attract the attention of nearby, non-raid zombies; Skeleton Armies force you to juggle both melee and ranged attackers at the same time; and Creeper Invasions pose the threat of creating massive craters everywhere!**|![Several zombies attacking](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/965f4a6c046acdeae5b162ff1dc45ff8b81f9bdf_0.webp) ![A big pile of goodies](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/13a31dbf941156deca2cc3e3c4229d5225d0e8c4.png)|**Depending on your difficulty, the raids will feature more or less waves. If you can beat all the waves and kill all the monsters, you'll find yourself with an abundence of sweet, sweet loot! Each new raid provides not only the main drop of the mob, but also a secondary resource! Raid zombies drop iron, skeletons drop diamonds, and spiders drop redstone!** *As a quick reminder, you can always right-click with any of the raid spawning items to give all raid monsters glowing.*
Spoiler As much as I've tried to fix it, monsters still get stuck in dumb holes and caves.
